message:
test(notifications): pin pipeline→sender Cx-forward contract (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Closes the observability gap at the
`NotificationPipeline → NotificationSender` boundary (parallel to the
tick 38 fix on the webhook dispatcher→transport boundary).

Before this change, every pipeline Cx-forward test used the
`MockSender` helper which only implemented `send()`. Its
`NotificationSender::send_with_cx` therefore fell through to the
trait default which delegates to `send()`. That meant a future
refactor of `NotificationPipeline::dispatch_payload_with_cx` that
regressed to calling `sender.send(...)` directly would be silent —
`sent.lock().len()` would populate identically in both cases.

This commit gives `MockSender` a second captured-payload bucket
(`cx_sent`) wired to a fresh `send_with_cx` override, gated on
`feature = "asupersync-runtime"`. A new
`MockSender::with_cx_sent(...)` constructor lets callers supply both
buckets; the existing `new(...)` constructor initializes `cx_sent`
to an empty `Vec` internally so every existing test continues to
compile unchanged.

New test `pipeline_handle_detection_with_cx_invokes_sender_cx_path`:

  * fans out across two senders,
  * drives `pipeline.handle_detection_with_cx(&cx, ...)`,
  * asserts `cx_sent.len() == 1` on each sender (cx-aware path
    invoked exactly once per sender),
  * asserts `sent.is_empty()` on each sender (ambient fallback
    untouched).

The existing `rate_limited_sender_with_cx_forwards_to_inner` test is
updated to use `with_cx_sent`. The assertion semantics strengthen —
previously it counted the `sent` bucket to check that exactly one
message got through (relying on the default-trait delegate). Now it
asserts `cx_sent.len() == 1` AND `sent.is_empty()`, proving both
(a) the wrapper forwarded through `send_with_cx` and (b) no fallback
to ambient `send` ever happened. This is strictly stronger.

Verification (local fork bypass):
  * notifications:: — 48 passed / 0 failed (incl. new test + updated
    rate_limited test)
  * cargo fmt -p frankenterm-core -- --check — clean
  * All pre-existing MockSender-based tests continue to pass (the
    non-cx tests hit `send()` → `sent` as before; only the cx-path
    tests now populate `cx_sent`).

Pairs with tick 38's webhook test to give end-to-end proof that
`NotificationPipeline::handle_detection_with_cx → sender.send_with_cx`
and `WebhookDispatcher::dispatch_payload_with_cx → transport.send_with_cx`
cannot silently regress to ambient paths.

Bead: ft-xbnl0.2.3

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
